page: Amazon Web Services m4.4xlarge is a general-purpose, fourth-generation server instance utilizing the Xen hypervisor. It is equipped with 16 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.4 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, a 45 MB L3 cache, and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average multi-core and memory performance alongside weaker single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores are categorized as weak, which is also reflected in weak single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds. Conversely, multi-core operations, such as the stress-ng multi-core test and Geekbench multi-core score, fall into the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests, including read-only small block cached transfers at 725.6 GB/sec and large block uncached writes at 43.64 GB/sec, consistently perform at average levels.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the m4.4xlarge is suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and database operations, where it achieves average benchmark ratings. It can also handle light, small-scale language model inference, though performance drops to weak levels for larger 7B models. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the older hardware generation offers a stable, dedicated resource profile for legacy workloads that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage or advanced AVX-512 instruction sets.
description: Amazon Web Services m4.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.4 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this Xen-hypervised server provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage or GPUs, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, such as a multi-core Geekbench score of 6224.0 and average memory bandwidth, though single-core performance remains weak. The instance is suitable for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, and database operations that require dedicated CPU resources without the need for high-speed local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m4.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, delivering balanced multi-core performance for standard workloads.

tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance offering balanced compute and memory resources for standard multi-core workloads without local storage.
